global.title: Powerhouse packages are published to NPM and installed into host apps via ph install
global.content:
Powerhouse packages follow a standard publishing workflow:

1. **Package naming**: Scoped convention `@your-org-ph/your-package-name` (the `-ph` suffix identifies Powerhouse ecosystem packages)
2. **manifest.json**: Declares package contents - document models (with document type IDs) and editors (linked to document types)
3. **Build**: `pnpm build` or `npm run build` compiles to dist/
4. **Publish**: `npm login` then `npm publish` (with `"publishConfig": { "access": "public" }` for scoped packages)
5. **Install**: `ph install @your-org-ph/your-package-name` makes document models and editors available in any Powerhouse environment

The `package.json` `files` array should include: `dist`, `manifest.json`, `document-models`, `editors`.

Semantic versioning is used for package versions. The AGPL-3.0-only license is common in the ecosystem.
global.description: Packages use scoped NPM names (@org-ph/name), include manifest.json, and are installed with `ph install @org/package`.
